title: Quantus: An explainable AI toolkit for responsible evaluation of neural network explanations
methodology: Toolkit paper. Implements published evaluation metrics from the XAI literature in a unified API; demonstrates on image-classification case studies.
contributions: Explanation quality is not self-evident: explanations should be evaluated against measurable properties (faithfulness, robustness, complexity, randomisation, axiomatic, localisation).
Quantus operationalises 30+ such metrics into a single toolkit.
Different XAI methods score very differently on different metrics — reporting a single number is insufficient.
weaknesses: Primarily targeted at neural network explanations and image data; tabular adaptation requires care.
The metrics themselves are contested (e.g., faithfulness has multiple competing definitions).
Reporting many metrics shifts the interpretation burden onto the reader.
emerald_relevance: Core citation for EMERALD-AI's section 5.11 explanation-fidelity validation. Moves the project past the assumption that an explanation is reliable simply because it was generated.
search_query: Quantus explainable AI toolkit JMLR 2023 Hedström
notes: Operationalises explanation evaluation: faithfulness, robustness, complexity, randomisation.
